31.reason: [isupper() returns non-zero for uppercase, not necessarily 1; should use isupper(a) without ==1]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [isupper() returns non-zero for uppercase, not necessarily 1; should use isupper(a) without ==1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [isupper() returns non-zero for uppercase, not necessarily 1; should check without ==1]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [isupper returns nonzero, not necessarily 1; should use if(isupper(a)) instead]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  条件判断不严谨。isupper 返回非零值但不一定是1，应改为 if(isupper(a))。
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  isupper returns non-zero, not necessarily 1. Comparing to 1 is logically incorrect. Use if(isupper(a))
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  isupper() returns non-zero for true, not necessarily 1. Should be if(isupper(a)).

59.reason: [Redundant null-termination after strcpy, and 'i' may be out of bounds if map exceeds 29 chars; also, strcpy already null-terminates, so this can corrupt data]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [i is not reset properly after processing a word; it still holds the length of the previous word, causing incorrect null termination]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [i is not reset properly after processing a word; it may have a stale value from previous word]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  i may exceed dc array size (30), causing buffer overflow. strcpy already null-terminates, making this redundant and unsafe.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Redundant if strcpy works correctly, and causes buffer overflow if i >= 30 since dc array size is only 30.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Redundant null-termination after strcpy, and 'i' may be out of bounds if map exceeds 29 chars; also, strcpy already null-terminates, so this can corrupt data]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Redundant null-termination after strcpy, and 'i' may exceed buffer size; also, strcpy already null-terminates, so this can corrupt data if i >= 30]

18.reason: [fgetc returns int; storing in char may lose EOF and cause undefined behavior]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [fgetc returns int; storing in char may lose EOF and cause undefined behavior]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  应改为 int a; 因为 fgetc 返回 int，char 无法正确存储 EOF（-1），会导致判断出错。
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  fgetc returns int, using char may fail to detect EOF correctly if char is unsigned. Should be int a;
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Should be 'int a;' because fgetc() returns an int, and comparing a char with EOF can lead to incorrect loop termination or infinite loops.
